description_long: This object is a pair of pliers with elongated, slightly curved handles and a robust metal construction. The handles are smooth and lack any visible ergonomic padding or texturing, suggesting a utilitarian design. The jaws are flat and squared off, with a unique open rectangular cutout at the gripping end, which is different from standard pliers that typically have pointed or serrated jaws. The pivot joint is a classic single-axis mechanism, allowing the jaws to open and close in a scissor-like motion. The overall finish is matte, with a uniform metallic gray color throughout. There are no visible brand markings or logos on any part of the tool.
description: A metallic pair of pliers with smooth, slightly curved handles and flat, squared jaws featuring a rectangular cutout. The tool is matte gray and lacks any visible branding or grip texture.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the smooth, slightly curved handles and the flat, squared jaws with a rectangular cutout.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the open jaws and the rectangular cutout, with the handles curving outward.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, similar to view 0, displaying the handles and the unique jaw design from the other side.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the open handles and the back of the jaws, emphasizing the tool's width and the pivot mechanism.
attribute_handle_type: Slightly curved, straight metal handles with a smooth surface and no visible ergonomic or insulated features.
attribute_jaw_design: Flat, squared jaws with a prominent rectangular cutout at the gripping end, lacking serrations or wire-cutting features.
attribute_pivot_mechanism: Single fixed pivot joint allowing standard scissor-like opening and closing of the jaws.
attribute_grip_pattern: Smooth metal with no visible grip pattern or texturing.
